metadata.title: DNG Camera Profile (DCP) Generator
metadata.formatDiscovery: CRITICAL FORMAT PIVOT: Real Adobe DCP files use BINARY TIFF tags (50700-52600), NOT XML in tag 50740. Story 9-1 implementation discovered this. Generation must write binary float32 arrays, SRational values, and ASCII strings directly to DNG tags. Tag 52552 (ProfileName) is OPTIONAL. See docs/stories/9-1-dcp-parser-FORMAT-PIVOT.md for complete details.

story.iWant: Recipe to generate valid DNG Camera Profile (.dcp) files from UniversalRecipe representation
story.soThat: I can convert my presets from other formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle) to Adobe camera profiles and use them in Lightroom, Camera Raw, and with DNG files
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Implement Tone Curve Generation (AC-3) - Implement universalToToneCurve() function generating 0.0-1.0 normalized float curve points - Ensure monotonic curve (output[i] >= output[i-1]) - Test with various parameter combinations
### Task 2: Generate Binary Tag Data (AC-2, AC-4) - Implement generateBinaryToneCurve() to convert ToneCurvePoint to binary float32 array - Implement generateColorMatrix() returning 9 SRational values (identity matrix) - Implement srationalArrayToBytes() for binary SRational conversion - Validate binary data lengths (tone curve: N*8 bytes, matrix: 72 bytes)
### Task 3: Create DNG File with Binary Tags (AC-1, AC-2) - Implement createDNG() function writing DNG magic bytes ("IIRC"/"MMCR") - Implement buildIFD() to create minimal IFD with standard TIFF tags - Write binary tags: 50708, 50721-50722, 50730, 50940, 52552 (optional) - Verify DNG magic bytes and IFD structure with binary tags
### Task 4: Implement Generate() Function (AC-1 to AC-4) - Implement Generate(recipe *universal.Recipe) ([]byte, error) - Add error handling for nil recipe input - Validate recipe parameters before generation
### Task 5: Write Unit Tests (AC-6) - Write 8 comprehensive unit tests covering binary format generation - Verify ≥85% test coverage for dcp/generate.go, dcp/tiff.go, dcp/profile.go - All tests pass in CI
### Task 6: Manual Validation in Adobe Software (AC-5) - Generate 3 test DCP files (neutral, portrait, landscape presets) - Test in Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom Classic - Document validation results in testdata/dcp/validation-report.md
### Task 7: Documentation (AC-1 to AC-5) - Add function comments for Generate() - Update docs/parameter-mapping.md with DCP binary generation mappings - Add README notes in testdata/dcp/

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-1: Generate Valid DNG/TIFF Structure** - Create TIFF/DNG file with proper header (II or MM byte order marker) - Use DNG version magic bytes ("IIRC" for little-endian or "MMCR" for big-endian) - Generate Image File Directory (IFD) with required binary tags - Include standard TIFF tags (ImageWidth, ImageLength, SamplesPerPixel, etc.) - Write TIFF/DNG file using github.com/google/tiff library or manual binary writing - Validate DNG structure (well-formed, valid byte order, correct version bytes) - Generated DCP files open without errors in TIFF/DNG readers
**AC-2: Generate Binary Profile Data Tags** - Write tag 50708 (UniqueCameraModel) as ASCII string - Write tag 50940 (ProfileToneCurve) as binary float32 array (input/output pairs) - Write tags 50721-50722 (ColorMatrix1/2) as 9 SRational values each - Write tag 50730 (BaselineExposureOffset) as SRational - Write tag 52552 (ProfileName) as ASCII string (OPTIONAL - only if non-empty) - Use correct binary data types (Float32, SRational, ASCII) - All tag data follows DNG 1.0-1.6 binary format
**AC-3: Map UniversalRecipe to Binary Tone Curve** - Generate tone curve as array of float32 (input, output) pairs normalized to 0.0-1.0 - Apply exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows transformations - Clamp all curve points to valid 0.0-1.0 range - Ensure monotonic curve (output[i] >= output[i-1]) - Write as binary float32 pairs (8 bytes per point: 4 bytes input + 4 bytes output)
**AC-4: Generate Binary Color Matrices** - Create ColorMatrix1 as 9 SRational values (identity matrix) - Create ColorMatrix2 with same identity values - Write as binary SRational (8 bytes each: 4 bytes numerator + 4 bytes denominator) - Identity matrix diagonal values = SRational{1, 1}, off-diagonal = SRational{0, 1}
**AC-5: Validate Generated DCP** - Validate DNG structure (magic bytes "IIRC"/"MMCR", IFD, required tags exist) - Validate binary tag data (correct lengths, valid SRational denominators) - Validate tone curve points (0.0-1.0 range, monotonic, float32 format) - Generated DCP loads in Adobe Camera Raw without errors - Generated DCP loads in Lightroom Classic without errors - Tone adjustments render correctly (visual spot-check)
**AC-6: Unit Test Coverage** - Unit tests for Generate() function with various inputs - Test edge cases and DNG/TIFF structure correctness - Test binary tag generation (float32 arrays, SRational values) - Test coverage ≥85% for dcp/generate.go, dcp/tiff.go, dcp/profile.go - All tests pass in CI

doc.title: Binary DNG Format Discovery
doc.snippet: Real Adobe DCP files use binary TIFF/DNG tags (50700-52600), NOT XML in tag 50740. Tag 50940 stores tone curve as float32 pairs (0.0-1.0). Tags 50721-50722 store color matrices as 9 SRational values. Tag 52552 (ProfileName) is OPTIONAL. DNG uses "IIRC" or "MMCR" magic bytes instead of standard TIFF version 42.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.snippet: Generate TIFF-based DCP file structure per Adobe DNG Specification 1.6. Write binary profile data in DNG tags (50700-52600 range). Map UniversalRecipe color parameters to DCP tone curve equivalents as binary float32 pairs. Generate required matrices as binary SRational arrays (identity matrices for non-calibration use). [REQUIRES UPDATE FOR BINARY FORMAT]

doc.title: Binary Tone Curve Generation Formulas
doc.snippet: 5-point tone curve generation with 0.0-1.0 normalized values: Start with linear curve {0.0, 0.0} → {0.25, 0.25} → {0.5, 0.5} → {0.75, 0.75} → {1.0, 1.0}. Apply exposure: Vertical shift of midpoint (0.5 → 0.5+exposure*0.25). Apply contrast: Steepen/flatten slope around midpoint. Apply highlights: Adjust top-end points (0.75-1.0 range). Apply shadows: Adjust bottom-end points (0.0-0.25 range). Convert to binary float32 pairs (8 bytes per point). [REQUIRES UPDATE FOR BINARY FORMAT]

doc.title: DCP Camera Profile Support (Epic 2)
doc.snippet: Generate TIFF-based DCP file structure per Adobe spec. Map UniversalRecipe parameters to binary DCP tags. Generate identity color matrices as binary SRational arrays (no camera calibration). Create tone curves from exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows as binary float32 pairs. Generated DCPs must load in Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom.

doc.title: Go 1.24+ Technology Stack
doc.snippet: Recipe uses Go 1.24+ with enhanced WASM support, Cobra CLI framework, and vanilla JavaScript for web interface. Hub-and-spoke architecture with UniversalRecipe intermediate representation. Zero external dependencies for core library.

artifact.reason: Binary DCP parser implemented in story 9-1. Shows reverse operation: extracting binary tags (50708, 50721-50722, 50730, 50940, 52552), converting float32 arrays to ToneCurvePoint, parsing SRational arrays to Matrix. Direct reference for generation (inverse operations).

Binary tone curve extraction from tag 50940. Shows how float32 pairs are stored (8 bytes per point), byte order conversion, normalization (0.0-1.0). Reference for generating binary tone curve data in reverse.
Binary color matrix extraction from tags 50721-50722. Shows SRational array format (9 × 8-byte values), numerator/denominator conversion. Reference for generating binary matrix data.

Tone curve analysis extracting exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows from curve shape. Shows inverse operation of generation - how tone adjustments map to curve points (0.0-1.0 range). Reference for understanding generation formulas.

Binary format structs: ToneCurvePoint with float64 Input/Output (0.0-1.0), SRational with int32 Numerator/Denominator, Matrix as [3][3]float64 array. DNG tag constants (TagProfileToneCurve=50940, TagColorMatrix1/2=50721/50722, TagProfileName=52552).
Core data structure with Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks fields that map to DCP tone curves. Reference for all tone adjustment parameters.
Example generator showing conversion from UniversalRecipe to format-specific parameters, including tone curve point conversion. Pattern to follow for DCP binary generation.

Python reference implementation showing TIFF tag constants and DCP structure. Note: This script may use XML format (tag 50740) which is INCORRECT for real Adobe DCPs. Use as reference for tag IDs only, NOT for format.

go_stdlib.purpose: Binary encoding for float32 arrays and SRational values
Buffer for DNG/TIFF binary writing operations
Pointer conversion for float32 to uint32 bits (if using manual binary writing)

external.purpose: TIFF read/write library for DCP container format. May be used for reading existing DCPs to understand format, but manual binary writing may be needed for custom DNG tags. Required because Go stdlib image/tiff is decoder-only and doesn't support custom tags.
external.status: NOT YET ADDED - must add to go.mod

internal.purpose: UniversalRecipe struct with tone adjustment fields
Types from story 9-1: ToneCurvePoint, SRational, Matrix, DNG tag constants

constraints.constraint: DCP files must follow Adobe DNG Specification 1.6 binary format for compatibility with Camera Raw and Lightroom
DNG magic bytes must be "IIRC" (0x49494352) for little-endian or "MMCR" (0x4D4D4352) for big-endian (NOT standard TIFF version 42)
Tag 50940 (ProfileToneCurve) must contain binary float32 array as (input, output) pairs, normalized to 0.0-1.0 range (NOT 0-255 integers)
Tags 50721-50722 (ColorMatrix1/2) must contain 9 SRational values each (72 bytes total per matrix)
Tag 52552 (ProfileName) is OPTIONAL - only write if non-empty string
All tone curve output values must be clamped to 0.0-1.0 range and maintain monotonic property (output[i] >= output[i-1])
Color matrices must be 3x3 identity matrices as SRational{1,1} for diagonal, SRational{0,1} for off-diagonal (Recipe doesn't perform camera calibration)
Binary data must use little-endian byte order (matching "IIRC" magic bytes)
Must use encoding/binary package for safe binary conversions (not manual pointer casting)
Test coverage target: ≥85% for generate.go, tiff.go, profile.go
Performance target: DCP generation <200ms (slower than other formats due to TIFF/DNG overhead)
Hub-and-spoke pattern: Generate() function takes UniversalRecipe as input, returns []byte DCP file
Manual validation required: Generated DCP must load in Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom Classic without errors
Round-trip validation: Generate → Parse (story 9-1) → Compare should preserve 95%+ accuracy

interface.name: Generate
interface.description: Main entry point for DCP generation. Takes UniversalRecipe and returns DNG file with binary tags as []byte. Orchestrates universalToToneCurve() → generateBinaryToneCurve() → createDNG() pipeline.

interface.name: universalToToneCurve
interface.description: Converts UniversalRecipe tone adjustments (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows) to 5-point DCP tone curve. Returns array of ToneCurvePoint with Input/Output as float64 values normalized to 0.0-1.0 range.

interface.name: generateBinaryToneCurve
interface.description: Converts ToneCurvePoint array to binary float32 array for tag 50940. Each point becomes 8 bytes (4-byte float32 input + 4-byte float32 output). Uses encoding/binary with LittleEndian.

interface.name: generateColorMatrix
interface.description: Returns 9 SRational values representing 3x3 identity matrix for ColorMatrix1/ColorMatrix2. Diagonal = SRational{1, 1}, off-diagonal = SRational{0, 1}. Used because Recipe doesn't perform full camera calibration.

interface.name: srationalArrayToBytes
interface.description: Converts SRational array to binary format for tags 50721-50722. Each SRational becomes 8 bytes (4-byte int32 numerator + 4-byte int32 denominator). Uses encoding/binary with LittleEndian.

interface.name: createDNG
interface.signature: func createDNG(toneCurve, colorMatrix1, colorMatrix2 []byte, profileName string) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Creates DNG file structure with binary tags. Writes DNG magic bytes ("IIRC" or "MMCR"), IFD offset, and IFD with binary tags (50708, 50721-50722, 50730, 50940, 52552 if non-empty). May use github.com/google/tiff or manual binary writing.

interface.name: buildIFD
interface.description: Creates minimal TIFF IFD (Image File Directory) with standard tags (ImageWidth=1, ImageLength=1, SamplesPerPixel=3, BitsPerSample=[8,8,8], PhotometricInterpretation=RGB). Used as container for DCP profile tags.

tests.standards: Testing follows Recipe's established patterns: table-driven tests with real sample files, round-trip validation, ≥85% coverage target. Uses Go standard testing package with subtests for granular failure reporting. Benchmark tests validate <200ms generation performance. Manual validation in Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom Classic required for AC-5 (visual spot-check that tone adjustments render correctly). Binary format validation: verify float32 arrays (8 bytes per point), SRational values (8 bytes each), DNG magic bytes ("IIRC"/"MMCR"), tag data lengths.

locations.location: testdata/dcp/ (36 real Adobe DCP files for round-trip testing, validation results)

test.description: TestGenerate_ValidRecipe - Generate DCP from populated UniversalRecipe with exposure +0.5, contrast +0.3, highlights -0.2. Validate DNG magic bytes (IIRC or MMCR), check IFD structure, verify binary tags 50940, 50721-50722 exist with correct lengths.

TestDNGStructure - Validate generated DNG has DNG magic bytes (not TIFF version 42), IFD with required tags (ImageWidth, ImageLength, SamplesPerPixel, binary profile tags). Use github.com/google/tiff to read back and verify (may need DNG version conversion).
TestGenerateBinaryToneCurve - Convert ToneCurvePoint array to binary float32. Verify length (N points × 8 bytes), byte order (little-endian), float32 values correct (0.0-1.0 range).
TestGenerateColorMatrix - Verify generateColorMatrix() returns 9 SRational values: diagonal SRational{1, 1}, off-diagonal SRational{0, 1}. Convert to bytes and verify 72-byte length.

TestBinaryTagData - Write binary tags to DNG, read back with parser from story 9-1. Verify tag 50940 (float32 array), tags 50721-50722 (SRational arrays), tag 52552 (ASCII string if present) are correctly formatted.

TestUniversalToToneCurve - Test tone curve generation with 0.0-1.0 normalized values: (1) Neutral (all zeros) - expect linear curve {0.0,0.0}, {0.25,0.25}, {0.5,0.5}, {0.75,0.75}, {1.0,1.0}. (2) Exposure +1.0 - expect midpoint shifted upward. (3) Contrast +0.5 - expect steeper slope. (4) Highlights -0.3, Shadows +0.2 - expect top/bottom points adjusted. Verify all outputs 0.0-1.0 and monotonic.
TestToneCurveClamp - Test extreme values (exposure +5.0, contrast +2.0) to ensure clamping to 0.0-1.0 range. Test negative extremes (exposure -5.0, contrast -2.0).
TestToneCurveMonotonic - Verify output[i] >= output[i-1] for all curve points (0.0-1.0 range) after applying all adjustments. Test with various combinations that might violate monotonicity.

TestSRationalConversion - Test srationalArrayToBytes() with identity matrix. Verify binary format: [1,0,0,0, 1,0,0,0] for SRational{1,1}, [0,0,0,0, 1,0,0,0] for SRational{0,1}. Total 72 bytes for 9 values.
TestGenerate_EmptyRecipe - Generate from UniversalRecipe with all zero values (neutral preset). Verify linear tone curve {0.0,0.0}...{1.0,1.0} generated, valid DNG structure.
TestGenerate_ExtremeValues - Test with extreme parameters: exposure +2.0, contrast +1.0, highlights +1.0, shadows -1.0. Verify valid DCP output with clamped values (0.0-1.0), no panics.

TestRoundTrip_DCP - Generate DCP → Parse with story 9-1 parser → Compare. Verify tone curve accuracy preserved (95%+ match, allow ±0.01 due to float32 precision). Verify metadata (profile_name) preserved.
TestOptionalProfileName - Generate DCP with empty profile_name (should omit tag 52552). Generate with non-empty profile_name (should include tag 52552). Verify both load in parser.
TestCoverage - Run 'go test -cover ./internal/formats/dcp/' and verify ≥85% coverage for generate.go, tiff.go (generation functions), profile.go (binary conversion functions).
BenchmarkGenerate - Benchmark DCP generation with typical UniversalRecipe. Target: <200ms (accept slower than other formats due to DNG/TIFF overhead with binary tag writing).

Manual Test - Adobe Camera Raw: Generate 3 test DCPs with binary format (neutral, portrait +exp/+contrast, landscape +sat/-highlights). Open in Camera Raw, verify no import errors, tone adjustments visible in histogram.

Manual Test - Lightroom Classic: Import same 3 binary format DCPs into Lightroom. Apply to test RAW image. Verify tone curve adjustments render correctly (visual spot-check before/after). Document results in testdata/dcp/validation-report.md with screenshots and version info.
